name: Cala Les Rotes — round trip from the port of Dénia
name_original: Cala Les Rotes — ida y vuelta desde el puerto de Dénia
region_intro: Jogging routes around Dénia offer a diverse range of terrain, from challenging mountain trails to flat greenways and sandy beaches. The region is characterized by the prominent Montgó Natural Park, which features significant elevation gains and panoramic views, alongside a varied coastline. Runners can explore well-maintained paths through orange groves or opt for coastal runs along the Mediterranean Sea. This varied geography provides options for different fitness levels and preferences. Best jogging routes around Dénia The most popular jogging route is Cala Les Rotes — round trip from the port of Dénia, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This route follows the scenic Las Rotas promenade, offering coastal views. Another top favourite among local runners is Running circular at Montgó from Denia, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through Montgó Natural Park, providing challenging ascents and expansive vistas. Local runners also love the Molinos de la Marina Alta — circular por la Ruta Torre del Gerro - Molins (marrón claro), a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical windmills, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Dénia is defined by mountainous trails in Montgó Natural Park, flat greenways, and extensive coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy runs to challenging ascents. The routes in Dénia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 5000 runners have used komoot to explore Dénia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Dénia?
A: Dénia offers a wide variety of running routes, with nearly 200 options to explore. These include everything from easy, flat paths to challenging mountain trails, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on Dénia's jogging trails?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrain for jogging in Dénia. Options include challenging mountainous trails within &lt;strong&gt;Montgó Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, flat and easy greenways like the &lt;strong&gt;Dénia Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, and extensive coastal paths along sandy beaches and rocky coves such as those found near Las Rotas.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia has several easy running routes, with over 20 options suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. The &lt;strong&gt;Dénia Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly ideal, offering a flat, well-maintained path through scenic orange and almond orchards.

Q: Can I find running routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging workout?
A: Absolutely. For a challenging workout, head to &lt;strong&gt;Montgó Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872461235/running-circular-at-montgo-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Montgó from Denia&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, offering demanding ascents and rewarding panoramic views of the coastline and surrounding areas.

Q: What are some scenic landmarks or natural features I can see while running in Dénia?
A: Dénia's running routes offer many scenic points. Along coastal paths, you'll enjoy stunning sea views and rocky coves. In &lt;strong&gt;Montgó Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, you can discover diverse flora, pine forests, and even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de l'Aigua&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Dénia Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; passes through picturesque orange and almond orchards, and you might even spot historical windmills on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1224858337/molinos-de-la-marina-alta-circular-por-la-ruta-torre-del-gerro-molins-marron-claro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de la Marina Alta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, many running routes in Dénia are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872461235/running-circular-at-montgo-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Montgó from Denia&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1224858337/molinos-de-la-marina-alta-circular-por-la-ruta-torre-del-gerro-molins-marron-claro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de la Marina Alta&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Dénia?
A: The running routes in Dénia are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 5,700 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain, stunning coastal views, and challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia offers several family-friendly options. The &lt;strong&gt;Dénia Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice due to its flat, traffic-free nature, making it safe and enjoyable for all ages. Many of the easier coastal paths are also suitable for families.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Dénia?
A: Dénia's warm and pleasant climate makes it suitable for jogging year-round. However, during the summer months, it's best to run in the early mornings or late afternoons to avoid the peak heat. Spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the sea or coast?
A: Absolutely. Dénia's extensive coastline provides numerous opportunities for scenic coastal runs. Routes along the &lt;strong&gt;Las Rotas promenade&lt;/strong&gt;, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1224872475/cala-les-rotes-round-trip-from-the-port-of-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Les Rotes — round trip from the port of Dénia&lt;/a&gt;, offer stunning sea views and a refreshing breeze. Many trails in &lt;strong&gt;Montgó Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; also provide breathtaking panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean.

Q: Can I combine jogging with exploring historical sites in Dénia?
A: Yes, you can. Dénia's old town and the area around &lt;strong&gt;Dénia Castle&lt;/strong&gt; offer unique urban routes that blend exercise with cultural exploration. You can jog through cobbled streets and enjoy panoramic views of the city, harbor, and sea, adding a historical dimension to your run.

Q: Are there any natural parks accessible for running near Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia is home to the prominent &lt;strong&gt;Montgó Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers numerous trails for trail running with varying difficulty levels. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;Dénia Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; can connect you to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5407844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, extending your running possibilities into a different natural landscape.
tips: On this side of Denia, there are some small beaches, but they're less accessible and have a lot of aquatic plants. (On the other side of Denia, there are large, clean beaches.)
It is a good place to watch the sunrise and change direction towards the center of Denia.
Steep little run up to the view point but well worth the effort a the top.
